summary: Iteration 2 — retest of Contact form 422 fix. All three requested checks PASS. Invalid-email path: app does NOT crash, [data-testid='contact-form-error'] renders a clean human-readable string ('value is not a valid email address: An email address must have an @-sign.'), the Sonner toast shows the same message, no React error overlay, no page-level errors, navigation to Home still works (page remains interactive). Happy path: form clears, [data-testid='contact-form-success'] renders, success toast 'Message sent. We will be in touch shortly.' shown. Backend regression via curl: POST /api/contact returns 201 for valid payload and 422 for invalid email. The HIGH bug from iteration 1 is resolved.

issues: (carried over from iter 1, minor) Stacked headline spans concatenate without whitespace for screen-reader/SEO snapshots — visual layout unaffected.

critical_code_review_comments: Contact.jsx submit() catch handler now correctly normalises FastAPI 422 detail (array of {msg}) into a comma-joined string before setState — fix verified working.
Form still uses noValidate by design — backend remains the single source of truth for email validation; acceptable since the error UI is now safe and readable.
seed_data_creation: 1 valid TEST_-prefixed contact submission posted to MongoDB during retest (happy-path verification). No cleanup endpoint exists.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Contact form invalid-email crash is fixed and verified. Backend POST /api/contact behaves as expected (201/422). No outstanding issues. Only remaining note is the minor cosmetic Home-hero whitespace-in-headline accessibility note from iteration 1.
rca of the issue: Resolved. Fix in /app/frontend/src/pages/Contact.jsx (lines 39-50): detail = err?.response?.data?.detail; msg = Array.isArray(detail) ? detail.map(d => d?.msg || String(d)).join(', ') : (typeof detail === 'string' ? detail : fallback). Verified end-to-end with Playwright on the production preview URL.
